refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To be atomic and solution-neutral, these should be separated into distinct FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite analysis.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide detailed analysis data specifically for the terrain of the worksite, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital representation of the terrain using spatial data abstraction
Create a feature extraction process to identify and classify key site features
Develop a terrain analysis module that models terrain characteristics relevant to planning and execution
Produce a worksite safety overlay that integrates operational policies with terrain data
Provide an interface to share the generated worksite model/map with planning and execution functions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital representation of the terrain using spatial data abstraction

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data generation with feature extraction and safety overlays
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a DP focused on generating a digital terrain representation supports independence by separating data generation from feature extraction and policy integration, which can be modularized separately.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of sufficient spatial data to generate accurate terrain models
Risk that operational policies may require frequent updates, potentially impacting the stability of the terrain model if tightly coupled
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the digital terrain representation in agreed formats
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly decomposing the DP into separate modules for terrain data generation, feature extraction, and safety policy integration to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for processing and generating digital terrain representations from spatial data

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives spatial data and sensor inputs relevant to terrain mapping

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends generated digital terrain data to other system components or external systems

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain generation is a core function of NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion since data inputs and outputs are well encapsulated.
Low containment; Vehicle module focuses on physical control and movement, not data abstraction or terrain modeling.
Some involvement in terrain data usage for remote control, but not responsible for generation.
Minimal relevance; safety services use terrain data but do not generate it.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the digital terrain generation DP because it specializes in processing spatial data and generating digital representations, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input which may affect module performance.
Interface changes in Telemetry or Network Link could impact module integration.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital representation of the terrain using spatial data abstraction' directly addresses the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by specifying a method to analyze terrain through digital modeling, fulfilling the functional requirement.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk noted with a diffusion score of 2 for the NGHE Solution module, indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within a single module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment (score 8) for the DP, with minimal diffusion. Other modules have low containment and higher diffusion scores, confirming that the DP is not scattered across multiple modules and is well localized within the NGHE Solution module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the acceptance criteria specify that a worksite model/map must be generated and available to planning/execution functions, which can be tested by checking the availability and correctness of the digital terrain data output by the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to the NGHE Solution module for implementing the DP, with well-defined external interfaces (Telemetry input and Network Link output) supporting data flow and integration.

recommended_revisions: Monitor dependencies on Telemetry data input to ensure timely and accurate data delivery to the NGHE Solution module.
Establish interface versioning and change management processes for Telemetry and Network Link interfaces to mitigate integration risks.
Consider documenting fallback or error handling strategies within the NGHE Solution module for cases of delayed or corrupted spatial data input.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification, minimal diffusion risk, high containment, and clear verification feasibility. The DP directly addresses the FR, and the IM allocation is clear and well-contained within a single module. Recommended revisions are minor monitoring and interface management tasks rather than fundamental decomposition changes. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear, well-contained design without unnecessary further decomposition reduces complexity and risk.

revise.why: Implement recommended monitoring of Telemetry data input dependencies, interface versioning, and error handling strategies to mitigate integration and data delivery risks.

assumptions: Telemetry data input will be timely and accurate as monitored.
Interfaces will be managed with versioning and change control processes.
open_risks: Potential delays or corruption in spatial data input affecting terrain modeling.
Integration risks from interface changes without proper versioning.
evidence_needed: Verification tests confirming availability and correctness of digital terrain data output.
Monitoring reports on Telemetry data input timeliness and accuracy.
Documentation of interface versioning and fallback/error handling strategies.
